Definition: used to introduce the information or input in a problem statement
Example: Given a list of email addresses, return a collection containing each email only once while preserving the first-seen order.

Definition: to produce an answer as the result of a function or problem
Example: Given a non-negative integer, return the sum of its decimal digits using recursive thinking.

Definition: the data that is provided to a program or task
Example: Input: emails = ["a@test.com", "b@test.com", "a@test.com"]

Definition: the result produced from the input
Example: Output: ["a@test.com", "b@test.com"]

Definition: a statement that makes the result or reasoning clear
Example: Explanation: duplicates are removed and the remaining names are sorted.

Definition: rules that limit the size, form, or valid values of the input
Example: Constraints: 0 <= names.length <= 100000; names[i] is non-null; sort by natural string ordering.

Definition: to keep something unchanged while processing data
Example: Preserve first-seen order.

Definition: the arrangement or sequence in which items appear
Example: Given validation errors in the order they were detected, return them exactly in that order, including duplicates.

Definition: the sequence based on when each item first appears during scanning
Example: Given a list of email addresses, return a collection containing each email only once while preserving the first-seen order.

Definition: a value that appears more than once
Example: Explanation: duplicates are removed and the remaining names are sorted.

Definition: different from all others; not repeated
Example: Contract: each distinct word appears exactly once as a key.

Definition: existing only once within a set or result
Example: Given a list of names, return unique names sorted alphabetically.

Definition: one time that something appears
Example: "a@test.com" appears twice, but the first occurrence order is preserved.

Definition: the number of times something appears
Example: Why use: practice using HashMap for frequency counting.

Definition: a relationship that connects one value to another, often key to value
Example: Given a list of usernames in login order, return a mapping from username to number of logins.

Definition: a limit value used for comparison or decision-making
Example: Given a list of integer readings and a threshold, return the first index whose value is greater than or equal to the threshold.

Definition: the position number of an element in a sequence
Example: Given a list of strings and an index, return the item at that index.

Definition: using numbering that starts at 0 instead of 1
Example: Contract: return the item stored at the provided zero-based index.

Definition: arranged in a specific order, usually increasing or alphabetical
Example: Return unique names sorted alphabetically.

Definition: going from smaller to larger or from A to Z
Example: Contract: output is unique and sorted ascending by natural string order.

Definition: going from larger to smaller or from Z to A
Example: Sort users by age descending, then by name.

Definition: the set of values between two limits
Example: Return entries whose keys fall inside a half-open range.

Definition: an interval that includes one end but excludes the other
Example: Problem: Given integer keys and values, return all entries whose keys are inside [fromKey, toKey).

Definition: the process or order of visiting items in a structure
Example: Given a graph as an adjacency list and a start node, return nodes in breadth-first traversal order.

Definition: visiting all nearby nodes before moving deeper
Example: Traverse a graph in breadth-first order.

Definition: the level of nesting or distance downward in a structure
Example: Given a string containing parentheses and other characters, return the maximum nesting depth of balanced parentheses.

Definition: correctly matched or evenly arranged according to the rules
Example: Return -1 if the parentheses are unbalanced.

Definition: not correctly matched or not in the required form
Example: Return -1 if the parentheses are unbalanced.

Definition: meeting all required rules or conditions
Example: Given a list of scores, return only scores between 0 and 100 inclusive.

Definition: treating uppercase and lowercase letters as different
Example: Matching is case-sensitive.

Definition: not equal to null; having a real value
Example: Constraints: 0 <= names.length <= 100000; names[i] is non-null.

Definition: done by modifying the original data structure instead of creating a separate result
Example: Remove duplicates in-place.

Definition: the value or goal that a process is trying to find or reach
Example: Find a profile by target ID.

Definition: the beginning part of a word, string, or sequence
Example: Find the longest common prefix.

Definition: the ending part of a word, string, or sequence
Example: Compare a prefix and a suffix of the same string.

Definition: a continuous part of a string
Example: The first and last characters match, then the inner substring also matches.

Definition: a sequence that keeps the original order but may skip some elements
Example: Check whether one string is a subsequence of another.

Definition: next to each other with no gaps in between
Example: Find the longest contiguous subarray with the required sum.

Definition: to leave something out of consideration or processing
Example: Ignore non-parenthesis characters.

Definition: not more than one time
Example: Visit each node at most once.

Definition: an ordered collection of elements stored and accessed by position
Example: Output: [{name: "C", age: 30}, {name: "A", age: 20}, {name: "B", age: 20}]

Definition: a sequence of characters treated as one text value
Example: Given a string, return true if it reads the same forward and backward using recursive boundary checks.

Definition: a whole number with no fractional part
Example: Given a non-negative integer, return the sum of its decimal digits using recursive thinking.

Definition: a structure made of nodes connected by edges
Example: Given a graph as an adjacency list and a start node, return nodes in breadth-first traversal order.

Definition: a single point or element in a graph, tree, or linked structure
Example: Visit each node at most once.

Definition: next to or directly beside something else
Example: Count the number of adjacent equal characters.

Definition: two related items considered together
Example: Given key-value pairs loaded from a config file, return a map that preserves the order in which keys appear.

Definition: a secondary rule used when the primary comparison gives the same result
Example: Contract: age is the primary order and name is the tie-breaker.

Definition: done as one complete operation that cannot be interrupted partway
Example: Refactor check-then-act cache initialization into an atomic ConcurrentHashMap operation.

Definition: at the same time or during overlapping periods of execution
Example: multiple threads may request the same key concurrently; the value creation function should be called only when needed.
